executiveSummary: The ADE - Trade semantic model (45 tables, 801 measures, 31 relationships) drives 1.24 million daily queries against Databricks, with 5.1% exceeding 10 seconds and 0.8% exceeding 30 seconds. The main fact table (fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1, ~116.5 GB, DirectQuery) is a hub with degree 21 and generates the bulk of query load at 42,764 queries/day. The slowest queries read up to 6 billion rows (19.6 GB) to return fewer than 5,000 rows — a scan amplification ratio exceeding 1,000,000:1. Real PBI-generated SQL confirms all 91 columns are selected from the fact table even when visuals need only 1-2 aggregated values. 49 critical-complexity DAX measures generate 14+ SQL subqueries each via nested CALCULATE and FILTER(ALL) patterns. 77 measures use FILTER(ALL) which forces full table scans. The #1 recommendation is to create a narrow serve view with only the ~23 columns referenced by PBI, combined with refactoring the top FILTER(ALL) measures to use KEEPFILTERS or explicit column filters.

topFindings.title: Column Over-Selection on Fact Table (91 cols vs ~23 needed)
topFindings.description: PBI DirectQuery selects all 91 columns from sales.serve.fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 even when a visual only needs 1-2 aggregated values (e.g. stock_quantity). The serve view is a direct SELECT * from the curated table with no column reduction. This generates 42,764 queries/day reading an average of 2.5s each, with P95 at 10s. The slowest queries read 19.6 GB per execution.
topFindings.recommendation: Create a narrow serve view serve_fact_trade_daily_narrow_v1 in dbt containing only the ~23 columns actually referenced by PBI measures: dim_date_sk, dim_product_option_sk, dim_pricing_store_sk, dim_web_store_sk, dim_shop_sk, dim_warehouse_sk, dim_country_sk, dim_territory_sk, dim_selling_channel_sk, dim_seller_sk, dim_price_status_sk, dim_price_status_group_sk, dim_inventory_status_sk, dim_product_lifecycle_status_sk, dim_lifecycle_price_status_sk, dim_stock_age_band_sk, financial_year_week_id, plus the measure columns (retail_sale_quantity, retail_sale_value, stock_quantity, etc.). Then update the PBI partition expression to point to the new view.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 60-75% reduction in read_bytes per query. Query time reduction from avg 2.5s to ~0.8-1.2s.
tradeoffs: Requires dbt deploy cycle to create new view
Must ensure all DAX measure references are included in narrow view

options.name: Option A: Narrow serve view
options.description: Create serve_fact_trade_daily_narrow_v1 with only PBI-referenced columns
options.pros: Low effort, no PBI logic changes, largest single improvement
options.cons: Requires dbt deploy + PBI source update

options.name: Option B: Column projection in M query
options.cons: Harder to maintain, still reads full view server-side

topFindings.title: FILTER(ALL) Anti-Pattern in 77 Measures Forcing Full Table Scans

measures: Total Retail Sales Buy Value - LY

topFindings.description: 77 DAX measures use FILTER(ALL(...)) which overrides all filters and forces DirectQuery to scan the entire fact table on every evaluation. Combined with context transitions (avg 7-8 per critical measure), the top 49 critical-complexity measures generate an estimated 14 SQL subqueries each. This is the primary driver of the 5-6 billion rows read in the slowest queries.
topFindings.recommendation: Refactor the top 20 FILTER(ALL) measures to use KEEPFILTERS or explicit column-level filters. For WTD/MTD/HTD/YTD measures, replace FILTER(ALL(Date), Date[financial_year_week_id] = ...) with CALCULATE([Base Measure], KEEPFILTERS(Date[financial_year_week_id] = ...)). For ratio measures like 'Retail Sales Units Conversion % LY', use DIVIDE with a pre-computed denominator variable. Prioritise the 49 critical-complexity measures first.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 50-80% reduction in rows scanned for affected measures. P95 query time reduction from 10.2s to ~3-5s.
suggestedAnalysisSteps: Export top 20 FILTER(ALL) measures and run in DAX Studio with Server Timings
Identify which FILTER(ALL) patterns can be replaced with KEEPFILTERS
Test WTD measures with explicit time intelligence filters
tradeoffs: DAX refactoring requires careful testing of each measure to ensure functional equivalence
Some FILTER(ALL) patterns may be intentional for ratio calculations

topFindings.title: Date Filter via JOIN Instead of Direct WHERE on Fact Table
topFindings.description: PBI applies date predicates by JOINing dim_date_v2 with WHERE calendar_date = '2026-04-12', then joining to the fact table via dim_date_sk surrogate key. Databricks cannot push this filter down to the fact table's liquid clustering on dim_date_sk because the predicate is on a column in a different table. The fact table (116.5 GB) is scanned without cluster pruning, reading billions of unnecessary rows.
topFindings.recommendation: Option A: Add a calculated column to the fact table with the actual calendar_date (or a calendar_date filter column) and create a direct relationship, enabling PBI to filter directly on the fact. Option B: Create a parameterised M query that applies a date WHERE clause before the fact table is loaded. Option C: Add a WHERE filter on the serve view using a date range parameter. The clustering on dim_date_sk is present but ineffective because PBI never sends a direct predicate on this column.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 90%+ row reduction on date-filtered queries. Queries that read 4.4B rows should drop to ~10-50M rows.

connectionModeComparison.directQuery: Current — date filter via JOIN, no cluster pruning, 4.4B rows scanned
connectionModeComparison.hybrid: Aggregation table for common date ranges in Import mode, DQ fallback for ad-hoc dates
connectionModeComparison.parameterFilter: M-level date parameter reduces fact scan to current period only

tradeoffs: Calculated column approach increases model complexity
M-level filtering requires report authors to use the date parameter

topFindings.title: 3 Bidirectional Cross-Filter Relationships on DirectQuery Tables

relationships: Option ↔ Product Option In Pricing Store

topFindings.description: Three bidirectional cross-filter relationships exist in the model. In DirectQuery mode, bidirectional filtering causes additional SQL subqueries because Databricks must evaluate the filter in both directions. The Option table (dual, degree 9) is involved in all three, amplifying the impact.
topFindings.recommendation: For each bidirectional relationship, evaluate whether bidirectional filtering is needed: (1) Option ↔ Option Ranking — if only used for ranking display, switch to single-direction and use CROSSFILTER() in specific measures. (2) Option ↔ Product Option In Pricing Store — evaluate if single-direction suffices. (3) Option ↔ Product Option In Store — same evaluation. Use CROSSFILTER(Option[dim_product_option_sk], 'Product Option In Store'[dim_product_option_sk], BOTH) in specific measures instead of model-level bidirectional.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 10-20% reduction in SQL subquery count for queries touching these relationships.

subFindings.reason: Bidirectional needed for ranking cross-filter in pivotTable visuals
subFindings.recommendation: Use CROSSFILTER() in DAX measures that need reverse filtering

subFindings.relationship: Option ↔ Product Option In Pricing Store
subFindings.reason: Enables filtering pricing store by product option
subFindings.recommendation: Evaluate if single-direction suffices — pricing store typically filters options, not vice versa
Switch to single-direction with DAX CROSSFILTER() where reverse is needed

tradeoffs: Removing bidirectional may break existing visual cross-filtering — test each page

topFindings.title: 49 Critical-Complexity DAX Measures Generating 14+ SQL Subqueries
topFindings.description: 49 measures have critical complexity (score 44-45) driven by 7-8 context transitions and 5-6 FILTER(ALL) calls each. Each generates an estimated 14 SQL subqueries in DirectQuery mode. These are predominantly WTD/MTD/HTD/YTD time intelligence measures that nest multiple CALCULATE layers with FILTER(ALL(Date)).
topFindings.recommendation: Consolidate time intelligence into shared base measures. Create a single WTD base measure using CALCULATE with KEEPFILTERS, then reference it from all WTD variants (Retail Sales Value WTD, Retail Return Value WTD, etc.). This reduces context transitions from 7-8 to 2-3 per measure. Use VAR/RETURN pattern to avoid repeated subexpression evaluation.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 60-70% reduction in SQL subquery count for time intelligence measures.
suggestedAnalysisSteps: Group the 49 critical measures by pattern (WTD, MTD, HTD, YTD, LY)
Create a template refactoring for each pattern
Test in DAX Studio with Server Timings before and after
tradeoffs: Large refactoring effort — 49 measures to update
Functional equivalence must be verified for each

topFindings.title: 12 Report Pages Exceed 15 Visuals (up to 28 per page)
topFindings.description: All 12 active pages in the Trade report exceed the recommended 15-visual threshold. Topline Performance has 28 visual containers. Each visual generates its own DirectQuery SQL query against Databricks, meaning opening the Topline Performance page fires up to 28 simultaneous queries. With the fact table averaging 2.5s per query, page load can exceed 30 seconds.
topFindings.recommendation: Split the heaviest pages into sub-pages or use bookmarks to show/hide visual groups. For Topline Performance: move the detail matrix to a drill-through page. For pages with 20+ visuals, identify visuals that share the same measure and consolidate into fewer, more informative visuals. Use 'show items with no data' sparingly as it multiplies query load.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 40-60% reduction in concurrent queries per page load, improving perceived load time from 30s to 10-15s.

topFindings.title: All Pages Use Time Period Slicer but No Direct Date Range Slicer
topFindings.description: All 12 pages in the Trade report have a Time Period slicer (advancedSlicerVisual bound to Time Period.Time Period), which filters by financial periods like 'Last Week' or 'Current Month'. Pages like Topline Performance, Weekly Trade Overview, and Seasonality Overview rely solely on this relative time filter. While this is functional, the Time Period filter applies via JOIN to dim_financial_time_period_v1 and then to dim_date_v2 — it does not directly constrain the fact table, so cluster pruning is still limited (see F3). The 'Date' variant pages (e.g., Topline Performance Date, Weekly Trade Overview Date) add explicit Calendar Date slicers for more granular control.
topFindings.recommendation: No urgent action required — the Time Period slicer is already present and functional. For incremental improvement, consider adding a default selection to the Time Period slicer (e.g., 'This Week') so users always start with a filtered view rather than 'All'. The more impactful fix is F3 (direct date filtering on the fact table).
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Minimal standalone — this is a usability improvement. The real date filtering fix is F3.

topFindings.title: Query Reduction Settings Not Optimised on Trade Report
topFindings.description: The Trade report has cross-highlighting enabled and slicer/filter 'Apply' buttons disabled. In DirectQuery mode, cross-highlighting generates additional queries each time a user hovers over a visual (the engine must re-query related visuals). Without 'Apply' buttons, every slicer or filter selection immediately fires queries instead of batching them. Auto-refresh is NOT enabled — this is a query reduction settings issue, not an auto-refresh issue.
topFindings.recommendation: In PBI Desktop → File → Options → Report settings → Query reduction: (1) Disable cross-highlighting to prevent hover-triggered queries. (2) Enable 'Add an Apply button to each slicer' so users can select multiple slicer values before queries fire. (3) Enable 'Add an Apply button to each filter' for the same reason. The 'Apply all slicers at once' option is already enabled, which is good.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 5-15% reduction in interactive query volume during user sessions.

topFindings.title: 19 Wide Tables/Matrices in Trade Report

visuals: 19 wide tableEx/pivotTable/matrix visuals across multiple pages

topFindings.description: 19 visuals in the Trade report use wide tables or matrices. Each column in a matrix generates a separate column projection in the DirectQuery SQL, and combined with the fact table's 91 columns being selected regardless, this multiplies the data processed.
topFindings.recommendation: Review wide matrix visuals and reduce column count where possible. Use drill-through pages for detailed breakdowns instead of showing all dimensions in a single matrix. Consider Top N filters on matrices to limit row cardinality.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 10-20% reduction in query complexity for pages with wide matrices.

topFindings.title: 8 Dual Mode Tables That Could Be Import
topFindings.description: 8 financial transform/time intelligence tables are in Dual mode but contain relatively static reference data (financial calendar mappings). In Dual mode, PBI uses DirectQuery when the table is joined with a DQ table, adding unnecessary JOINs to Databricks queries. Since these are small lookup tables, switching to Import would cache them locally and eliminate Databricks roundtrips.
topFindings.recommendation: Switch the 8 financial transform tables to Import mode. They contain static calendar/financial period mappings that change infrequently. Set a scheduled refresh (e.g., daily at 06:00). This removes 8 tables from DirectQuery SQL generation.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 5-10% reduction in query complexity by removing financial lookup table JOINs from Databricks SQL.

topFindings.title: Cross-Catalog JOINs Across 5+ Databricks Catalogs
topFindings.description: A single PBI visual generates SQL that JOINs tables from 5 Databricks catalogs (sales, technology, product, supplychain, sourcingandbuying) in a single query. Cross-catalog JOINs prevent some query optimisations and increase planning overhead.
topFindings.recommendation: This is an architectural characteristic of the model — not easily changed without restructuring the data platform. Mitigate via narrower serve views (F1), switching small dims to Import (F10), and reducing the number of tables per query via report design improvements (F6).
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Indirect — primarily addressed via F1, F6, and F10.

topFindings.title: 6 Engineering BPA Findings: Functions on Filter Columns (E05)

dbtModels: 6 dbt models with functions applied on filter/join columns

topFindings.description: 6 dbt models apply functions on columns that are used in WHERE or JOIN predicates (e.g., CONCAT_WS on surrogate keys, CAST on date columns). Functions on filter columns prevent predicate pushdown and disable clustering benefits.
topFindings.recommendation: Review the 6 flagged models and remove function wrapping on filter/join columns. For computed composite keys (like dim_product_option_in_pricing_store_sk = concat_ws('|', ...)), consider pre-computing in the curated layer instead.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 5-15% improvement in cluster pruning efficiency for affected tables.

topFindings.title: 54 Nested CALCULATE Patterns in DAX Measures

measures: 54 measures with nested CALCULATE (each nesting adds a SQL subquery level)

topFindings.description: 54 measures contain nested CALCULATE expressions. In DirectQuery, each CALCULATE nesting generates an additional level of SQL subquery. Combined with FILTER(ALL), these create the 4-5 level deep subquery patterns observed in real Databricks query history.
topFindings.recommendation: Flatten nested CALCULATE by using VAR/RETURN pattern. Instead of CALCULATE(CALCULATE([Measure], filter1), filter2), use VAR filtered = CALCULATE([Measure], filter1, filter2) RETURN filtered. This reduces subquery depth.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 1-2 fewer SQL subquery levels per measure, reducing planning and execution time.
suggestedAnalysisSteps: Identify the 10 most-frequently-used nested CALCULATE measures
Profile in DAX Studio to measure actual subquery count
Refactor using VAR/RETURN and test equivalence

topFindings.title: 650 Unused Column Candidates Across Model

tables: Multiple tables — 650 columns not referenced by any measure or relationship

topFindings.description: BPA identifies 650 columns that are not referenced by any DAX measure, relationship, or calculated column. In DirectQuery, unreferenced columns still appear in the SELECT list generated by PBI, increasing read_bytes. Hiding or removing these columns reduces the SQL width.
topFindings.recommendation: Review the 650 unused column candidates and hide columns that are genuinely unnecessary. Focus on the DirectQuery tables first (Daily Trade has 91 columns — many are surrogate keys or audit columns not used by reports). This works in combination with F1 (narrow serve view) for maximum impact.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Incremental — combined with F1, reduces read_bytes by 60-75%.

actions.action: Optimise query reduction settings: disable cross-highlighting, enable slicer/filter Apply buttons
Create narrow serve view serve_fact_trade_daily_narrow_v1 with only ~23 PBI-referenced columns
Set default Time Period slicer selection to 'This Week' on Topline Performance, Weekly Trade Overview, and Seasonality Overview pages
actions.location: Trade / Topline Performance, Weekly Trade Overview, Seasonality Overview

actions.action: Switch 8 financial transform tables to Import mode with daily scheduled refresh

actions.action: Remove bidirectional filtering on Option ↔ Product Option In Pricing Store and Option ↔ Product Option In Store
Refactor top 20 FILTER(ALL) measures to use KEEPFILTERS pattern
Consolidate WTD/MTD/HTD/YTD measures into shared base measure pattern
actions.location: @Measures (WTD: Retail Sales Value WTD, Retail Return Value WTD, etc.)

actions.action: Flatten 54 nested CALCULATE measures using VAR/RETURN
Split Topline Performance page (28 visuals) into summary + detail drill-through
Reduce visual count on Weekly Trade Overview and Seasonality Overview (26 each)
actions.location: Trade / Weekly Trade Overview, Seasonality Overview

actions.action: Review and optimise 19 wide matrix visuals — add Top N filters and reduce columns
Hide 650 unused columns in semantic model, prioritising DirectQuery tables
actions.location: Daily Trade, Option, and dimension tables

actions.action: Evaluate aggregation table for common date-range queries (current week, current month)
Review functions on filter columns in 6 dbt models (E05 findings)
Investigate direct date filtering mechanism to enable cluster pruning on fact table
